content: We can reframe multi-LLM agent architectures to have each individual LLM agent be augmented by an ICM curiosity mechanism that generates intrinsic rewards for the agent when it encounters ambiguous or novel unstructured information that its base LLM cannot confidently parse, rather than only relying on task-based extrinsic rewards from a top-level central controller. This creates a self-organizing multi-agent system where curious agents spontaneously specialize in under-explored problem domains without explicit manual assignment of roles.
